competitors.name: Rao's (Campbell's Sovos Brands)
competitors.action: Featured prominently in The Taste Panel's March 20 YouTube taste test alongside DiGiorno, Screamin' Sicilian, Amy's, and Newman's Own — praised for 'restaurant-quality taste' and zero artificial additives. Campbell's acquired Sovos Brands (Rao's parent) in 2024; Rao's frozen pizza now part of a major CPG portfolio with full distribution muscle.
competitors.notes: Rao's now has Campbell's distribution and marketing scale while retaining 'authentic Italian' premium brand equity. Occupies same premium-clean-label niche as Real Dough. Their moat: Italian-restaurant legacy. Real Dough's moat: living sourdough fermentation + craft baker story. Both are fighting for the same $15+ frozen pizza shopper — understand their shelf adjacency in key accounts.

competitors.name: Conagra / Celeste Pizza
competitors.action: Celeste Pizza for One (Conagra) received a California Prop-65 lead-content notice in 2026 — formal legal filing for lead contamination in product.
competitors.notes: Legal/safety vulnerability for a major frozen pizza SKU. While Celeste is value-tier, this is a reputational vector Real Dough can exploit — further differentiates clean, honest-ingredients positioning. Worth monitoring if it spreads to other Conagra pizza lines.

competitors.name: Nestlé / Tombstone
competitors.action: Tombstone Original Thin Crust 5 Cheese won Food & Wine's 'best overall' frozen pizza taste test (March 2026), outperforming 16 competitors including premium brands.
competitors.notes: Value brand winning a major editorial taste test is a signal — Tombstone is getting press without spending on premium ingredients. Real Dough needs to ensure it's getting submitted to these media taste tests. A win in Food & Wine or Sporked is high-leverage earned media.

competitors.name: Conagra Brands
competitors.action: Committed $450 million capital expenditures for FY2026 focused on supply chain resilience and portfolio shift toward frozen foods and snacking categories. Signals major bet on frozen as growth category.
competitors.notes: Conagra going heavy on frozen infra (Birds Eye, Marie Callender's, Healthy Choice, Slim Jim). This raises the competitive bar in distribution capacity, shelf placement negotiation, and promotional spending. Larger retailers will prioritize Conagra shelf space if they're committing $450M — Real Dough needs differentiated retailer pitch and demand-side pull from consumers.

competitors.name: Dr. Oetker
competitors.action: Suprema sourdough range (UK, Oct 2025) tracking toward potential U.S. rollout — no confirmed date. Referenced in multiple March 2026 market reports as benchmark for premium frozen pizza formulation.
competitors.notes: Previously reported March 28. Continues to be the benchmark competitive threat. Triple-fermented sourdough positioning is direct Real Dough territory. U.S. launch timeline still unknown — monitor quarterly.

competitors.name: Yough
competitors.action: Greek yogurt frozen pizza now actively on shelf in ~2,000 Target stores after March 17 launch. Early consumer reception being tracked.
competitors.notes: Previously reported March 28. Monitor consumer reviews and shelf velocity over next 4-6 weeks — functional pizza either breaks through or fades fast at Target.

trends.topic: Tariff-driven ingredient cost inflation affecting frozen CPG
trends.magnitude: Wisconsin businesses paid $3.9B in tariffs March 2025–Jan 2026. Food-at-home index +2.4% YoY Feb 2026. Beef +9.8% YoY, eggs +4.1%.
trends.context: Broad tariff pressure is raising costs across frozen food supply chains. Italian staples (olive oil, cheese, cured meats) face import tariff exposure. This is a margin compression threat for every frozen pizza brand — but could also justify Real Dough's premium price point as consumers see conventional brands raise prices to compensate.

trends.topic: GLP-1 / Ozempic macro headwind for frozen pizza
trends.magnitude: 12.4% of U.S. adults now on GLP-1 drugs. Pizza/pasta among categories with reduced cravings. Chips/snacks -10.1%, sweet bakery -8.8% among GLP-1 users.
trends.context: Growing GLP-1 adoption is reducing overall food consumption among a significant cohort, with pizza/pasta specifically called out as craving-reduced categories. This is a macro headwind for the entire frozen pizza category. Counterstrategy: Real Dough's clean-label, better-ingredient story appeals to 'treat myself thoughtfully' mindset — GLP-1 users still indulge, just less frequently and more intentionally. Premium positioning may be more durable than volume-driven brands.

trends.magnitude: Private label hit all-time high dollar sales (year ending Dec 2025). Simultaneously, premium frozen meals growing at premium price points. 40% of consumers switching to cheaper brands on staples while trading up on experiences.
trends.context: The frozen food market is bifurcating: shoppers buy store-brand staples to save money AND buy premium frozen meals as affordable 'treat yourself' moments. Real Dough sits squarely in the 'affordable luxury' slot this bifurcation creates — consumers who cut streaming services still want a great pizza night. This is the core value proposition to articulate in retail pitch decks.

trends.topic: Frozen pizza editorial taste test media cycle
trends.magnitude: Multiple major taste test features in March 2026 alone: Food & Wine (winner: Tombstone), The Taste Panel YouTube (16 brands reviewed), Daily Voice NJ (Screamin' Sicilian #3).
trends.context: Consumer media outlets are running frozen pizza taste tests at higher frequency — representing significant earned media opportunity. Real Dough is not appearing in any of these rankings yet. Submitting product to Food & Wine, Sporked, and major YouTube food channels should be a near-term marketing priority.

trends.magnitude: Frozen ready meals market shifting competitive axis — premium segment leading growth. $43.9B frozen cooked meals market in 2025 growing to $46.36B in 2026.
trends.context: Industry analysts explicitly noting the frozen category is shifting from 'price-per-calorie' to 'flavour-per-serving' competition. This reframe is extremely favorable for Real Dough — living sourdough, quality ingredients, and elevated flavor are the new competitive currency.

trends.topic: Grocery shelf space expansion for frozen foods
trends.magnitude: +15-20% more frozen meal shelf space allocated YoY 2025→2026 by supermarkets
trends.context: Previously reported (Mar 28). Supermarkets continuing to dedicate more linear feet to frozen — favorable for Real Dough's retail expansion conversations.

market_data.metric: US frozen food manufacturing industry revenue 2026

breaking.headline: Conagra / Celeste Pizza hit with Prop-65 lead content notice in 2026
breaking.summary: Celeste Pizza for One (Conagra) formally flagged under California Prop-65 for lead content. Legal/safety vulnerability for a mass-market frozen pizza brand.

breaking.headline: GLP-1 drugs now at 12.4% U.S. adult adoption — pizza named as craving-reduced category
breaking.summary: Macro headwind for frozen pizza category. Pizza/pasta explicitly cited in GLP-1 research as reduced-craving foods. 135B weight loss sector is disrupting food purchasing patterns at scale.
